product_name: Guìhuā Niàng Dòufu (Osmanthus Fermented Tofu Milk Tea)
reason: Flagship product with documented sensory appeal — described as creamy, non-beany, melt-in-mouth tofu paired with aromatic osmanthus and fermented rice wine notes; widely available across active branches in Chengdu, Hangzhou, and Hainan [4].

Specifically designed for young and student demographics with ultra-affordable pricing, flexible school-adjacent locations, and viral product differentiation (tofu-based texture); reported popularity among middle and high schoolers as well as undergraduates [3].

product_name: 产品身份证系列饮品（如低糖/零卡茶咖）
reason: Strategically dominant in campus markets—now in nearly 200 universities—with transparent health metrics ('Health Calculator'), cultural appeal, and record-breaking student engagement (e.g., 2200+ cups sold on opening day at Tsinghua) [3].
Globally recognized, consistently priced under $4–$5, widely available near campuses, and frequently cited among top accessible boba brands for students [6].

product_name: Signature Brown Sugar Boba Milk Tea
reason: Strong campus footprint, value-focused combos and student discounts, frequently ranked among top boba chains by US university reviewers [5].
Recommended for affordability and group-serving capacity—ideal for student gatherings and school events in markets like Houston [5].

A commercially established, widely available boba-adjacent beverage that substitutes traditional tapioca pearls with house-made soy tofu pudding — soft, neutral-flavored, high-protein, and vegan-friendly. It’s the brand’s flagship product and has driven rapid nationwide expansion across China [profile_md].
Official flagship product with documented consumer preference, silken tofu integration, and balanced nutritional profile; aligns with young, health-conscious, and value-driven demographics.

Explicitly founded around 'tofu milk tea' as its flagship innovation; widely reviewed as a top-performing, differentiated product with strong appeal among students and youth; supported by scalable franchise model and regional dominance.
Core signature product — a soy-based milk tea with pudding-like texture, widely available across 300+ stores in下沉 markets; delivery availability confirmed via regional food delivery platforms.
Pudding is explicitly recommended by Gong cha as a silky, custard-like addition best paired with Taro Milk Tea — aligning well with soy pudding’s smooth mouthfeel [3].
Documented as available warm and praised for its balanced, comforting profile — ideal for delivery and seasonal appeal [5].
Combines black tea, herbs, and soy milk pudding — offers functional depth and pairs naturally with fruit-forward or citrusy additions [4].

product_name: Brown Sugar Boba + Soy Pudding Milk Tea
reason: Brown sugar boba adds chewy contrast and rich caramel notes that enhance rather than clash with soy pudding’s mild umami-sweetness [4].
Affordable flagship product with strong presence near schools and commercial streets; explicitly designed for high-traffic, youth-centric locations and priced for mass affordability.

Signature innovative product combining silky tofu texture with tea and milk; priced accessibly (¥6–¥12), widely available via delivery in tier-2/3 cities and campuses; uniquely bridges health-consciousness, novelty, and affordability.

Offers non-traditional flavor combinations (e.g., 'Peach Grove + Oolong', 'Light Green + Rose') in convenient, home-brewable triangular tea bags; ~¥4.3/pack, highly customizable (cold/hot/milk-added), and aligned with Gen-Z taste exploration.

product_name: 冰鲜柠檬水 / 蜜桃四季春
reason: Budget staples (¥4–¥6) with fresh-fruit-forward profiles; 'Honey Peach Siji Chun' delivers authentic fruit pulp and light floral tea — simple but distinct from standard boba formulas.
Ethnically distinct, savory-sweet profile rarely seen in mainstream bubble tea; 400g pack for ¥66, yielding ~20+ servings (~¥3.3/serving); ready-to-mix and culturally authentic.

product_name: 蒙古老奶茶粉（无蔗糖、咸口）
reason: Traditional Mongolian-style dairy-tea blend with roasted notes and natural saltiness — a bold, non-sweet alternative; compact, portable, and priced at ¥29.7 for 300g (~¥0.1/g).

Innovative tofu-based formulation offers a creamy, low-fat, subtly sweet alternative to traditional boba; widely praised by students and young consumers across下沉 markets in Fujian, Guangdong, and Sichuan; over 300 stores nationwide attest to its popularity and repeat appeal.

A uniquely positioned, high-value-for-money product targeting students and young adults; leverages tofu’s plant-based creaminess, low-cost scalability, and strong differentiation in the crowded bubble tea space—validated by rapid nationwide expansion (>300 stores) and consistent praise from teens and undergraduates [summary][evidence].
